title: iva frutescens subsp. oraria
intro: Iva frutescens subsp. oraria, also known as marsh elder or sea marshelder, is a plant species in the family Malvaceae. It is a shrub or small tree that grows in coastal areas, particularly in salt marshes and mangrove swamps. The medicinal properties of Iva frutescens subsp. oraria are attributed to its leaves, roots, and stems, which have been used in traditional medicine for their anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral properties. The health benefits of this plant include reduced inflammation, improved wound healing, and relief from respiratory issues, and it can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and salves for its medicinal properties.
